description: Thin material made from pulp, used for writing or printing; the surface that receives marks. 'Shena' names what waits empty—potential before the first word.
sound_symbolism: The word sheets thin. 'She' /ʃe/ spreads with gentle whisper, surface forming. Then 'na' rests flat and stable, paper ready.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'lo shena' (the paper). Receives marks from 'phari' (pen) or 'sheli' (brush). Symbol of potential.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Paper carried knowledge—the precious sheet that preserved thought across time and distance.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Emptiness as potential—blank paper holds infinite possibility before the first mark.
